title: A method of identifying a productive interval along a well
abstract: Methods and systems are disclosed that include obtaining (905) well data along an interval of a well and identifying (910) a first candidate interval within the interval using the well data. The well data includes acoustic data and porosity data. A formation surrounding the well within the first candidate interval includes micropores. The methods further include determining (915) mechanical parameter data along the first candidate interval based on the acoustic data and identifying (920) a second candidate interval within the first candidate interval based on the mechanical parameter data and the porosity data. The methods still further include determining (925) water saturation data along the second candidate interval based on the well data, identifying (930) a productive interval within the second candidate interval based on the water saturation data and a water saturation threshold, and determining (935) a completion plan for the well based on the productive interval.
claims:
Claims ATTORNEY DOCKET NO.18733-1095WO1; CLIENT REF. NO. SA91095 CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: obtaining (905) well data (400) along an interval (335, 405) of a well (205), wherein the well data (400) comprises acoustic data (415) and porosity data (420); identifying (910) a first candidate interval (600) within the interval (405) using, at least in part, the well data (400), wherein a formation (210) surrounding the well (205) within the first candidate interval (600) comprises micropores (100); determining (915) mechanical parameter data (500) along the first candidate interval (600) based, at least in part, on the acoustic data (415); identifying (920) a second candidate interval (625) within the first candidate interval (600) based, at least in part, on the mechanical parameter data (500) and the porosity data (420); determining (925) water saturation data (800) along the second candidate interval (625) based, at least in part, on the well data (400); identifying (930) a productive interval (820) within the second candidate interval (625) based, at least in part, on the water saturation data (800) and a water saturation threshold (815); and determining (935) a completion plan for the well (205) based, at least in part, on the productive interval (820). 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising completing the well (205) based, at least in part, on the completion plan, wherein the completion plan comprises information on where to induce hydraulic fractures within a reservoir (315) associated with the well (205) based on a location of the productive interval (820). 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein identifying (910) the first candidate interval (600) comprises: obtaining a nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) log along the interval (405) of the well (205), 

ATTORNEY DOCKET NO.18733-1095WO1; CLIENT REF. NO. SA91095 wherein the well data (400) further comprises the NMR log (430); and identifying the first candidate interval (600) within the interval (405) where the NMR log (430) is below a transverse relaxation time threshold (435). 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the transverse relaxation time threshold (435) is between 200 milliseconds and 300 milliseconds, inclusive. 5. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein identifying (910) the first candidate interval (600) comprises: obtaining rock cores (215) along the interval of the well (205); generating, using scanning electron microscopy, an image of each of the rock cores (215), wherein the image of each of the rock cores (215) is of pores; and identifying the first candidate interval within the interval where the image of each of the rock cores (215) is of the micropores (100). 6. The method of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the second candidate interval (625) within the first candidate interval (600) is identified (920) where a mechanical value among the mechanical parameter data (500) is below a mechanical parameter threshold (520) and a porosity value among the porosity data (420) is above a porosity threshold (525), wherein the mechanical value and the porosity value are associated to a position (410) within the second candidate interval (625). 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the mechanical parameter data (500) comprises Youngâs modulus data. 8. The method of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein identifying (920) the second candidate interval (625) comprises: determining, using a differential effective medium (DEM) model, a model (720) between a mechanical parameter and porosity for a pore aspect ratio (725); and identifying the second candidate interval (625) within the first candidate interval (600) where a mechanical value among the mechanical parameter data (500) is within a mechanical parameter threshold (520) of the model (720) and a porosity value 

ATTORNEY DOCKET NO.18733-1095WO1; CLIENT REF. NO. SA91095 among the porosity data (420) is within a porosity threshold (525) of the model (720), wherein the mechanical value and the porosity value are associated to a position (410) within the second candidate interval (625). 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the pore aspect ratio (725) is between 0.1 and 0.2, inclusive. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the formation (210) surrounding the well (205) within the second candidate interval (625) comprises euhedral texture and/or subhedral (115) texture. 11. The method of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the water saturation data (800) is determined using an Archie equation. 12. The method of any one of claims 1 to 11, wherein the water saturation threshold (815) is greater than 50%. 13. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the formation (210) surrounding the well (205) within the productive interval (820) comprises carbonate. 14. A system comprising: a computer system (1005) configured to: receive (905) well data (400) along an interval (335, 405) of a well (205), wherein the well data (400) comprises acoustic data (415) and porosity data (420), identify (910) a first candidate interval (600) within the interval (405) using, at least in part, the well data (400), wherein a formation (210) surrounding the well (205) within the first candidate interval (600) comprises micropores (100), determine (915) mechanical parameter data (500) along the first candidate interval (600) based, at least in part, on the acoustic data (415), identify (920) a second candidate interval (625) within the first candidate interval (620) based, at least in part, on the mechanical parameter data (500) and the porosity data (420), 

ATTORNEY DOCKET NO.18733-1095WO1; CLIENT REF. NO. SA91095 determine (925) water saturation data (800) along the second candidate interval (625) based, at least in part, on the well data (400), and identify (930) a productive interval (820) within the second candidate interval (625) based, at least in part, on the water saturation data (800) and a water saturation threshold (815); and a production management system (1050) configured to: determine a completion plan for the well (205) based, at least in part, on the productive interval (820). 15. The system of claim 14, further comprising a hydraulic fracturing system (1100) configured to complete the well (205) based, at least in part, on the completion plan, wherein the completion plan comprises information on where to induce hydraulic fractures within a reservoir (315) associated with the well (205), based on a location of the productive interval (820). 16. The system of claim 14 or 15, further comprising a well logging system (300) configured to obtain (905), at least in part, the well data (400). 17. A non-transitory computer-readable memory having computer-executable instructions stored thereon that, when executed by a computer processor (1008), perform steps comprising: receiving (905) well data (400) along an interval (335, 405) of a well (205), wherein the well data (400) comprises acoustic data (415) and porosity data (420); identifying (910) a first candidate interval (600) within the interval (405) using, at least in part, the well data (400), wherein a formation (210) surrounding the well (205) within the first candidate interval (600) comprises micropores (100); determining (915) mechanical parameter data (500) along the first candidate interval (600) based, at least in part, on the acoustic data (415); identifying (920) a second candidate interval (625) within the first candidate interval (600) based, at least in part, on the mechanical parameter data (500) and the porosity data (420); 

ATTORNEY DOCKET NO.18733-1095WO1; CLIENT REF. NO. SA91095 determining (925) water saturation data (800) along the second candidate interval (625) based, at least in part, on the well data (400); identifying (930) a productive interval (820) within the second candidate interval (625) based, at least in part, on the water saturation data (800) and a water saturation threshold (815); and determining (935) a completion plan for the well (205) based, at least in part, on the productive interval (820). 18. The non-transitory computer-readable memory of claim 17, wherein identifying (910) the first candidate interval (600) comprises: receiving a nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) log along the interval (405) of the well (205), wherein the well data (400) further comprises the NMR log (430); and identifying the first candidate interval (600) within the interval (405) where the NMR log (430) is below a transverse relaxation time threshold. 19. The non-transitory computer-readable memory of claim 17 or 18, wherein the second candidate interval (625) is identified (920) where a mechanical value among the mechanical parameter data (500) is below a mechanical parameter threshold (520) and a porosity value among the porosity data (420) is above a porosity threshold (525), wherein the mechanical value and the porosity value are associated to a position (410) within the second candidate interval (625). 20. The non-transitory computer-readable memory of claim 17 or 18, wherein identifying (920) the second candidate interval comprises: generating, using a differential effective medium (DEM) model, a model (720) between a mechanical parameter and porosity for a pore aspect ratio (725); and identifying the second candidate interval (625) within the first candidate interval (600) where a mechanical value among the mechanical parameter data (500) is within a mechanical parameter threshold (520) of the model (720) and a porosity value among the porosity data (420) is within a porosity threshold (525) of the model (720), wherein the mechanical value and the porosity value are associated to a position (410) within the second candidate interval (625).
